other_observers: David Dowell, who concurred with ID
specific_location: Andrick Ponds SWA, pond off of MCR 2
description: A Mallard-type duck, darker-bodied than any Mexican Duck I've seen. Entire body blackish-brown, including vent. Head and neck contrastingly straw colored, particularly pale on chin and cheek. Eyestripe fairly faint and crown not as dark as that of Mexican Duck or Am Black Duck. Speculum blue-ish in color, with barely visible pale rear-border to the speculum (seen only if you squint at a couple of the photos). Underwing coverts bright white and unmarked. The bill color looks olive in some photos, and at least partly orange in others, which is confusing.... I also think that I can see the dark spot near the gape in some photos, but this could be artifact. Size and shape similar to that of Mallards. Tail as dark as body.Behavior: Loafing with Mallards, then flying off with themCall: not heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow
